DashboardIntro: The company, read from the enforced types — the same page for everyone. Every figure below is read from the system, never authored here.
PulseCaption: Every number above is read from the proved types — click any card.
BoardCaption: Each card's ‹ › walks one step to a what-if page — the system still holds its real status until a typealias changes and the build re-proves it.
CardsIntro: The named team's own cards — identity, tasks, and a walk into the real access, one degree of who you are.
EmployeesIntro: The directory, read from the roster. The named team owns the documents; the rest of the company scales the same policy.
DepartmentsIntro: The four organizational units, read off the types. Each carries a shared space anyone in it may view, and two carry an owned vault with a named owner.
DocumentsIntro: Every document in the company, read off the types — two owned vaults and four shared spaces. Access to each owned vault is listed principal by principal, read from the compiler's verdict.
TasksIntro: Every task in the company, read off the types. The same tasks lay out as a kanban on the board.
BoardIntro: A board laid out the way the dashboard is: columns of cards, each an org type. Every assignee links into the directory.
ReportsIntro: The company's audit, re-proved on every build. A policy that contradicted itself would not compile, so every figure below is true as of this build.
DataResidencyLine: Every owned document has an owner inside its own department, so data stays where it belongs.
ManagerLedLine: Every owned document is led by a manager.
OwnersLine: Finance vault owner: {financeOwner} in {financeDept}. Engineering repository owner: {engineeringOwner} in {engineeringDept}.
InvariantsProvedLine: invariants proved, counted off the type.
ScaleIntro: Access follows the role, not the person, so the whole company resolves from a few certified rules.
ReturnToOfficeIntro: The office-arrangement policy, read off the types: which arrangements clear the office minimum, and which do not.
WhyArrangementHeading: Why the arrangement, not the hours
RefusalWarningPrefix: The third arrangement is not merely listed as "no". Compile
RefusalWarningMiddle: as office-compliant and the build refuses: her `Site` is
RefusalWarningSuffix: , and the office-minimum conformance has no instance over `Remote` to satisfy. The refusal names the rule, not just the person.
CyclesIntro: Forced distribution: every review cycle, rank the company and let the bottom share go. This demo does not rank or score anyone — it takes the decision as given and enforces the part that holds by construction: the year cannot close until the cut is recorded.
InterlockHeading: The interlock: closing the year requires a cut
InterlockNote: The next year is Closed<Prev, Cut> — a type you cannot write without a cut committed for that year. A cut that names a different band will not compile, so the size is proved, not just stated.
DirectoriesIntro: Every attribute is a directory: a protocol whose conforming types are its atoms. Open a directory to see its values, and open a value to see what it conforms to and every sibling beside it. Each link lands on the type's own DocC page, so the web you walk is the type lattice itself.
GuestAccessLine: You're a guest. Sign in above to see what this identity reaches.
PathHeldPrefix: ●●●●  ·  the path held. You walked the only sequence that exists for
PathHeldSuffix: , so it resolved to one identity, |S| = 1.
WhatYouReachSuffix: reaches, now that the identity is yours.
PersonHeroIntro: Identity, role, and access, drawn from the same fields listed below. The named team's own card carries the sign-in walk; every other card's access is read off role and department alone.
